reviews.review_msg: He posts all his recorded lecture videos to Canvas so don't bother going to his class. He is very hard to understand (German accent and quiet). Found it easier just use the videos. Exams were semi difficult and some questions were strange. Be prepared to reteach yourself most class topics

I had Norbert for Fall 2022. I felt he was a good prof who seemed quite caring about his students. He curved like crazy too, and though the tests were hard all the required knowledge was covered during lecture. I'm doing this now because I see he has a low rating and I wanna bump it up.
Does not care about students, won't answer emails. Fails everyone until the end of the course when he is forced to curve students.

does not answer student emails or listen to feedback from anyone, even TAs. exams are insane. everybody comes fairly close to failing the course until he is forced to curve it at the end of the semester.

I almost failed the class, as it's my first year and i didnt know how to study for this. For Norbert's tests, listen to what he says, not whats on the board because what he says is on the test. Also, try to get the best understanding of info. Don't bother reading the textbook. He's hilarious and kind, and wants the best for his students. 10/10 prof

he was bad at explaining the lectures and his tests were so hard. Half of his tests didn't make sense honestly. He structured them weirdly and it was hard to understand what he was asking. Also he is very quiet during the lectures and you can barely hear him. So make sure to seat underneath the speakers in the classroom to hear him better.

Haunerland is a pretty good prof! There is some issue with his accent being difficult to understand. He kept lectures interesting by singing every now and then. His exams and tests are all based on the lectures, which makes studying easier. Best advice: attend lectures because his explanation makes more sense in person.
He makes really specific questions for exams which were barely talked about in class.

His lecture notes are a little bit confusing and unorganized. Make sure to sit in the middle of the class because he's really soft spoken and has a heavy German accent. The midterms are difficult so study up, but if you combine the notes with the textbook you'll be okay!
Prof. Haunerland is a guy but do not take him for a first year biology class. 2nd/3rd year stuff in a intro biology class...
